item.title: Perfluorooctanoic Acid Exposure Disrupts Gut Microbiota and Aggravates Experimental Colitis.

item.title: Microbiome-driven alterations in tryptophan metabolism contribute to behavioral comorbidities in the Muc2 knockout mouse model of chronic colitis.

item.title: Colonoscopic and oral microbiota transplant therapy yield similar stool and neoterminal ileal engraftment profiles in a single-blind pilot randomized trial.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The intestinal microbiota play a key role in Crohn's disease (CD) and postoperative recurrence following ileocecal resection. Microbiota transplant therapy (MTT) could impact postoperative CD recur...

item.title: Urolithin A activates aryl hydrocarbon receptor-NLRP6-mediated pathways in intestinal epithelial cells to modulate mucosal immunity and strengthen gut barrier integrity.
item.description: Nature communications - The aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AHR) plays a central role in orchestrating gut barrier and mucosal immune functions in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Nevertheless, activation ...

item.title: Gut microbiome metabolites meet immunometabolism in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Trends in immunology - Review - Growing evidence indicates that gut microbiota-derived metabolites are key regulators of immunometabolism in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Intestinal epithelial cells and immune cells exhibit p...

item.title: Adaptive immune responses against Lachnospiraceae-derived flagellins and their role in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Mucosal immunology - Review - Adaptive immune responses, including B and T cell responses, play important roles in establishing and maintaining the mutualistic relationship between the host and intestinal microbiota. Host-micro...

item.title: Inhibition of Aryl hydrocarbon receptor Interleukin-22 signaling and worsening of intestinal inflammation by Sutterella species.

item.title: Modified Crohn's Disease Exclusion Diet and exclusive enteral nutrition (EEN) resolve oral dysbiosis in pediatric Crohn's disease: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Changes to the oral microbiome have been reported in patients with Crohn's disease (CD). The aim of this study was to determine the characteristics and dynamics of the oral microbiome in children r...

item.title: Habitual Ultra-processed Food Intake Is Associated with Gut Dysbiosis and Pro-inflammatory Metabolite Profiles in Korean Patients with IBD.

item.title: Tea and digestive system health: integrating gut microbiota-involved ADME and personalized nutrition for gastrointestinal disorders intervention.
item.description: Critical reviews in food science and nutrition - Review - The global burden of digestive diseases is escalating, marked by rising incidence and severity. Tea, a widely consumed beverage rich in polyphenols, offers a promising dietary intervention due to i...

item.title: Prophage induction contributes to alterations in the gut phageome during intestinal inflammation.
item.description: Cell reports - Bacteriophages (phages) are abundant members of the gut microbiota and regulators of bacterial communities. During homeostasis, gut phage communities are longitudinally stable and lysogenic replica...

item.title: High thrombin activity associated with crohn's disease induces microbiota pathogenicity contributing to mucosal inflammation.
item.description: Gut microbes - Thrombin is a serine protease produced by the intestinal epithelium that contributes to mucosal homeostasis by limiting microbial encroachment. In Crohn's disease, mucosal thrombin activity is mark...

item.title: Central Nervous System Involvement in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Bibliometric Analysis of Collaboration, Hotspots, and Research Trends.

item.title: Structural characterization and the protective effects of green walnut husk polysaccharides by modulating inflammation and gut microbiota for inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Zanthoxylum nitidum-derived active alkaloid nitidine chloride mitigates ulcerative colitis by remodeling gut microbiota and activating SIRT1 to restore intestinal barrier integrity.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a recurrent inflammatory bowel disorder with rising prevalence and limited efficacy of current treatments. Nitidine chloride (NC), a characteristic bioactive constituent ...

item.title: Unravelling the Critical Interplay of Oral and Gut Microbiota in Disease Development.

item.title: Mechanisms and therapeutic prospects of DNA methylation-mucosal innate immunity crosstalk in inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Curcumin-loaded tetrahedral framework nucleic acids reshape intestinal homeostasis in ulcerative colitis by coordinating barrier repair, microbiota reconstruction, and immune modulation.
item.description: Materials today. Bio - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) primarily driven by oxidative stress, sustained inflammation, barrier dysfunction, and gut dysbiosis. Current therapeutic drugs...
